title: Dog White Discharge from Vulva: Causes, Risks, and Emergency Care
meta_desc: Thick white discharge in dogs can indicate a life-threatening infection like pyometra. Learn why high urgency is required and how to triage.
content_html:
What is Thick White Discharge?

Thick white discharge from a dog's vulva is often a sign of an underlying health issue. In veterinary medicine, this can indicate several conditions, including vaginitis (inflammation of the vaginal canal) or, most seriously, pyometra. Pyometra is a severe, life-threatening infection of the uterus where pus accumulates. In some cases, it may also be associated with the end of a heat cycle or late-stage pregnancy, but it should never be ignored.

Is This an Emergency?

Urgency Level: High. If your dog is not spayed, thick white discharge is considered a medical emergency until proven otherwise. Pyometra can cause the uterus to rupture, leading to systemic sepsis and death. If the discharge is accompanied by lethargy, increased thirst, or vomiting, go to an emergency clinic immediately. Even in spayed dogs, this symptom requires a prompt veterinary exam to rule out severe urinary tract infections or stump pyometra.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

When contacting your veterinarian, taking a clear photo of the discharge and the surrounding area can be vital for triage. A visual record allows the medical team to assess the color, viscosity, and volume of the fluid before you clean your pet. This information helps the vet determine if your dog needs to be squeezed into the schedule immediately or if they can wait for a standard appointment.

faqs.question: Can my dog have pyometra if she was spayed?
faqs.answer: While rare, a 'stump pyometra' can occur if a small amount of uterine tissue was left behind during the spay surgery. All dogs with white discharge should be evaluated by a professional.

faqs.question: Are there other symptoms of pyometra to look for?
faqs.answer: Yes, common signs include drinking excessive amounts of water, frequent urination, loss of appetite, a bloated abdomen, and general weakness.

faqs.question: Could white discharge just be a normal part of her heat cycle?
faqs.answer: Discharge during a heat cycle is typically bloody or straw-colored. Thick white or cloudy discharge is usually indicative of pus and signifies an infection rather than a normal cycle.
